reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): validateDataColumn is registered as a validator in subscriber.go:316 and called by libp2p pubsub for all incoming data column sidecars - not dead code. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Attack path is via P2P gossip (untrusted), not requiring compromised trusted components. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct - hasSeenDataColumnIndex check at validate_data_column.go:157 occurs during validation, while setSeenDataColumnIndex call at subscriber_data_column_sidecar.go:42 occurs in the handler. These are separated by the pubsub pipeline. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by broadcasting two identical data column sidecars (same slot, proposer_index, index) to different network peers simultaneously. Both arrive at the victim node, both validators run concurrently (subscriber.go:424 shows handlers run as 'go pipeline(msg)'), both pass the hasSeenDataColumnIndex check (slot_aware_cache.go:31-34 uses RLock) before either handler marks the tuple as seen (slot_aware_cache.go:38-48 uses Lock), resulting in duplicate acceptance. The cache operations are individually thread-safe but the check-then-act pattern across validation and handler is not atomic. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-012 in file outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json states 'Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance' with assertion 'forall s1,s2: accepted(s1) && accepted(s2) && same_dedup_key(s1,s2) => s1 == s2'. The TOCTOU race violates this invariant. Gate 6 (Scope Check): TOCTOU race conditions in P2P validation are in-scope. Severity Calibration: Original severity MEDIUM. Impact: duplicate sidecars consume bandwidth and processing resources but do not cause consensus failure (downstream code handles duplicates). Target client is Prysm (31% CL share per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json). A single-client bug affecting 31% caps at Medium per severity thresholds, but actual impact is resource consumption on individual nodes (>0.01% network affected by broadcast amplification). Downgraded to Low per severity_classification: 'at least 0.01% of the network'. Prysm's 31% share means widespread exploitation could affect >0.01% but impact is limited to bandwidth/CPU DoS rather than consensus/slashing.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance.
